patches.find: a dimensional error that undermines the claim before we even assess the derivation. Even correcting the units charitably from rad/s² to rad/s, the dome's value 4.87×10⁻¹² falls 37% below Earth's measured precession angular velocity of 7.73×10⁻¹² rad/s — the prediction fails on value even after the unit correction is applied. The dome's own monitoring system (monitor.py) does not include gyroscopic precession in its 39-domain automated audit engine, confirming that even the model's author does not treat this as a computationally validated prediction. Even correcting the units charitably from rad/s² to rad/s, the dome's value 4.87×10⁻¹² falls 37% below Earth's measured precession angular velocity of 7.73×10⁻¹² rad/s — the prediction fails on value even after the unit correction is applied. The dome's own monitoring system (monitor.py) does not include gyroscopic precession in its multi-domain automated audit engine, confirming that even the model's author does not treat this as a computationally validated prediction.
patches.replace: a dimensional error that undermines the claim before we even assess the derivation. A defender might argue the rad/s² is intentional (predicting precession acceleration, not rate) — but VLBI and lunar laser ranging measure precession rate as effectively constant on human timescales, so a specific angular-acceleration value would imply a detectable changing precession rate that neither historical data nor modern geodesy records. Even correcting the units charitably from rad/s² to rad/s, the dome's value 4.87×10⁻¹² falls 37% below Earth's measured precession angular velocity of 7.73×10⁻¹² rad/s — the prediction fails on value even after the unit correction is applied. The dome's own monitoring system (monitor.py) does not include gyroscopic precession in its multi-domain automated audit engine, confirming that even the model's author does not treat this as a computationally validated prediction.
patches.rationale: Two changes combined: (1) Remove duplicate back-to-back sentences — the '39-domain' copy is deleted, keeping only the 'multi-domain' version per curmudgeon c3 recommendation (ISS-1842). (2) Insert preemptive escape-hatch sentence between the dimensional-error statement and the 'even correcting' sentence, closing the 'we meant acceleration not rate' defense (ISS-1843).
Update IAU citation from homepage to specific IAU 2006 Resolution B1 PDF. Flagged in c2 and c3 as unaddressed (ISS-1844).
